LAW AND MOTION 302, ASSIGNEE LANDMARK COLLECTION SERVICES INC.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. DEFENDANT MACALPIN'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN HIS BURDEN OF PROOF. (SEE CCP 703.580(B).) DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE LEVIES RELATE TO UNEMPLOYMENT BENEFITS, PAID EARNINGS, ETC. THERE IS NO TRACING. IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT'S FINANCIAL STATEMENT IS SUSPECT GIVEN HIS TOTAL MONTHLY EARNINGS OF $0 AND MONTHLY EXPENSES OF $4,641.19. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Jun-21-2024 Off Calendar

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Feb-27-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Gulllermo Macalpin, 415-990-3045, Defendant/Judgment Debtor.
John Bradley, Esq. 408-998-2000 Judgment Creditor/ASSIGNEE LANDMARK COLLECTION

Ruling - Argued. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

ASSIGNEE LANDMARK COLLECTION SERVICES INC.'s HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

Defendant Macalpin's claim of exemption is denied. Defendant failed to maintain his burden of proof. (See CCP 703.580(b).) Defendant fails to show that the levies relate to unemployment benefits, paid earnings, etc. There is no tracing. In addition, defendant's financial statement is suspect given his total monthly earnings of $0 and monthly expenses of $4,641.19.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Feb-02-2023 Continued To Feb-27-23 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Defendant/Judgment Debtor GUILLERMO MACALPIN in pro per;
No other appearances made in this matter.

ASSIGNEE LANDMARK COLLECTION SERVICES INC'S HEARING FOR DETERMINATION ON GUILLERMO MACALPIN'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION.

The Court notes that defendant Mr. Macalpin did not properly contest the Court's tenttive ruling and that the opposing side is not present.

The Court permits this matter to be continued to February 27, 2023 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.

The Court will notify the opposing side by email of the continued hearing date.

The Court reminds the defendant Mr. Macalpin that he must consult the tentative ruling to be posted the court day prior to the continued hearing date and follow all instructions contained within the posted tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***** After the hearing, the clerk emails attorney John F. Bradley Jr. as counsel for Assignee to inform Mr. Bradley of the February 27, 2023 continued hearing date. Mr. Bradley confirms receipt of the clerk's email per Mr. Bradley's email dated February 2, 2023. *****
